title: ipomoea hyoscyamoides var. melandrioides
intro: Ipomoea hyoscyamoides var. melandrioides, also known as moonflower, is a climbing vine native to Australia and Asia, characterized by its large, white, fragrant flowers that bloom in the evening. The plant contains tropane alkaloids, which exhibit medicinal properties, including sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. Consuming moonflower or its extracts may provide various health benefits, such as relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Traditionally, moonflower has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and promote relaxation and well-being.
